summary: CAD/PDF source QA is usable, but existing 3D exports are explicitly not a final modelling basis.

source_boards.note: Architectural shell, opening projection and wall cut readiness boards were removed because they depended on abstract projections rather than validated CAD-first worksets.

questions.title: Y01: panoramisch raam binnen boograam achtergevel
questions.question: Bronbewijs: Y01 blijft gekoppeld aan het boograam achtergevel.

questions.title: Y09/Y11: raam WC1 en raam berging gevalideerd
questions.question: Gebruikersvalidatie verwerkt: Y09 is raam WC1; Y11 is raam berging.

readiness_gates.name: wall vertical extents
readiness_gates.reason: Ground-floor plan gives wall footprint only; wall top/base levels must come from sections/elevations.

readiness_gates.name: exterior opening metric dimensions
readiness_gates.reason: 0 exact dimension slots are bound; do not cut windows/doors from boxes or marker locations.

readiness_gates.name: one-facade proof mesh
readiness_gates.reason: Only allowed after GATE-01 and at least one facade's exact opening slots are validated.

rejected_sources_not_used_for_mesh.reason: User rejected this layer because opening positions were wrong. It remains documented but is not an active geometry source.

readiness_gates.title: canonical repeated level references
readiness_gates.reason: The canonical level set is bound to repeated CAD/PDF facade evidence and exact source_entity_ids.

readiness_gates.title: wall vertical extents
readiness_gates.reason: The common reference levels are ready, but each wall/run still needs section/roof joins before extrusion.

readiness_gates.title: opening sill/header/threshold dimensions
readiness_gates.reason: Opening dimension slots are still unbound; windows and doors must not be cut from labels or boxes.

readiness_gates.title: material slots
readiness_gates.reason: User validated the material evidence. Slots are source-linked and editable, but not yet assigned to final mesh.

readiness_gates.title: exact CAD wall pieces grouped
readiness_gates.reason: All 89 validated wall segment polygons remain exact source CAD points with source_entity_ids.

readiness_gates.title: base/plinth references attached
readiness_gates.reason: 0.00 and -0.18 repeated level refs are linked as references; no extrusion uses them yet.

readiness_gates.title: wall top reference
readiness_gates.reason: 2.95 is linked as a repeated upper reference candidate, but final wall top needs section/elevation run matching.

readiness_gates.title: opening cuts
readiness_gates.reason: No opening cuts may be generated until exact width/height/sill/header/threshold slots are CAD-bound.

readiness_gates.title: black structural pieces
readiness_gates.reason: Structural/black pieces are stored as source CAD groups, not deleted and not merged with windows/doors.

readiness_gates.title: Opening labels and facade joins
readiness_gates.reason: 12 facade openings use the validated Y labels, facade roles and height-join contracts.

readiness_gates.title: Wall-run source context
readiness_gates.reason: 12/12 facade openings share original CAD source_entity_ids with wall-runs.

readiness_gates.title: Exact metric dimensions
readiness_gates.reason: 0 exact dimension slots are bound. Width/height/sill/header/threshold/frame/subdivision candidates remain source-linked hints only.

readiness_gates.title: Mesh cuts
readiness_gates.reason: No opening cut, frame, glass, sill, reveal or threshold mesh may be generated from this contract yet.

rejected_ground_floor_opening_wall_match.blocked_use: mesh generation, wall cuts, opening placement or exports

known_issues: uses bbox/component boxes for candidate visualisation
uses projected opening markers, not plan-confirmed wall cuts
model_generators.blocked_use: final editable or photorealistic house/parcel model

known_issues: uses line segments as rough wall strips
alignment is stair-center based rather than full source transform record
not validated against every elevation, section, opening and site object
model_generators.blocked_use: final editable or photorealistic house/parcel model

known_issues: not suitable as final editable/photorealistic basis
model_generators.blocked_use: final editable or photorealistic house/parcel model

blocking_rules_before_final_3d: Do not use legacy architectural shell/opening projection artefacts as active model geometry; they are removed from the active workflow.
Do not use bbox/cube candidate models as the final shell.
Do not use ground_floor_validated_wall_segments.json as master wall geometry; wall geometry must be rebuilt from original CAD hatches, polylines and source entity IDs.
Do not cut wall openings from normalized facade projection.
Do not cut wall openings from the rejected ground_floor_openings_matched_to_walls.json layer.
Do not cut wall openings until the numbered CAD plan opening/gap clusters are user-validated and mapped to the 12 facade opening contracts.
Do not generate opening meshes from Y markers; use ground_floor_validated_opening_plan_matches only after each contract is narrowed into exact CAD subroles and joined to height/elevation data.
Do not use user yellow markup as geometry; use it only to label and confirm preserved CAD entities/clusters.
Do not use the manually composed clean GLB as architectural truth.
Final model generation must consume CAD entity geometry/loops with source entity IDs attached.
Each generated selectable object must keep source role, layer and entity references.
Site/parcel mesh generation must consume validated_site_context and must exclude demolished old house and garage.
Driveway/oprit, karrenspoor, terraces, garden path, wadi, lowered zone, trees and ditch meshes must consume the user-validated visible_site_3d_scope; do not mesh directly from old H candidates.
Every joined object may reference the complete CAD detail catalog for facade, section, level, dimension and material detail.
Detail enrichment QA boards are source-pool validation aids only and are not exact object matches or mesh sources.
CAD detail components are candidate groupings only; original source_entity_ids remain the authority.
CAD text labels must be retained as metadata for popups, design/material context and QA traceability, even when hidden on default QA drawings.
Opening metric dimension contracts are source requirements only; plan bboxes and PDF crop rectangles are not final 3D sizes.
Every exterior opening mesh must wait for width, height, sill/header/threshold and subdivision evidence from CAD dimensions or validated facade/section details.
Use semantic facade roles from facade_source_mapping_audit; raw component filenames may be remapped and must not drive orientation or mirroring.
Do not use ground_floor_opening_dimension_preselection as dimensions or geometry; it is a shortlist only.
Do not use ground_floor_opening_dimension_human_review as dimensions or geometry; it is a readable review page only.
Do not treat ground_floor_architectural_shell_contract as a mesh; it is a CAD-first source contract with blocked wall/opening cuts.
Do not treat ground_floor_bound_level_constraints as a mesh; it binds level/material evidence only and still needs wall/roof/opening joins.
Do not treat ground_floor_wall_run_level_join_contract as mesh; it groups exact CAD wall pieces and level refs only, with opening cuts still blocked.
Do not treat ground_floor_wall_extrusion_contract as mesh; it is a non-mesh wall-source contract and all openings remain uncut.
Do not treat ground_floor_wall_opening_room_topology_contract as mesh; it only links exact CAD wall runs to opening and room-seed context.
Do not treat ground_floor_wall_graph_workbench as mesh; it is an exact CAD wall-edge graph for future room-cycle detection only.
Do not treat ground_floor_room_boundary_candidate_workbench as room geometry; it is side evidence around labels only and has zero closed or mesh-ready room boundaries.
Do not treat room_boundary_cycle_search_packets closed selected subgraphs as room polygons; they are search packets only and promote zero room boundaries.
Do not use ground_floor_room_portal_candidate_contract as walkable routing; it has zero walkable-ready portals and zero mesh-ready portals.
Do not use ground_floor_portal_side_resolution_work_order as routing; it is an agent-side blocker list with zero walkable-ready and zero mesh-ready portals.
Do not use ground_floor_portal_side_source_search_packets as routing; source overlaps are search evidence only and remain zero walkable-ready and zero mesh-ready.
Do not use ground_floor_portal_side_proximity_search_packets as routing or side binding; nearest-room proximity is search evidence only and remains zero walkable-ready and zero mesh-ready.
Do not use ground_floor_portal_side_evidence_matrix as routing or final side binding; it combines evidence but keeps zero final-bound, zero walkable-ready and zero mesh-ready portals.
Do not use ground_floor_portal_gap_pair_candidates as accepted doorway connectivity; pairs are crosscheck candidates only and remain zero final-bound, zero walkable-ready and zero mesh-ready.
Do not use ground_floor_portal_gap_edge_crosscheck_packets as accepted doorway connectivity; graph edges and topology runs are crosscheck candidates only.
Do not use ground_floor_portal_gap_edge_narrowing_work_order as accepted doorway connectivity; wall-run groups are narrowing evidence only.
Do not use room_editable_object_contract as geometry or material assignment; its slots are pending metadata until exact room boundaries and user/source material choices are bound.
Do not generate room floors, room volumes or room boxes while room_mesh_blocker_matrix reports zero mesh-ready rooms.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_wall_run_dimension_bindings as mesh; it links openings to wall-run context but still has 0 exact bound metric slots.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_3d_preparation_contract as mesh; it separates original CAD source IDs into 3D candidates/preserved context/metadata but still has 0 exact bound metric slots.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_metric_slot_bindings as mesh; it only candidate-binds strong CAD dimension-chain evidence to editable metric slots.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_3d_readiness_contract as mesh; it is a blocking contract and must report 0 mesh-ready openings until missing CAD slots and subgeometry are bound.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_plan_trace_subgeometry dominant extents as final clear opening widths; they are original CAD trace evidence for subrole narrowing only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_plan_subrole_workbench candidate roles as final frame/glass/wall-cut roles until they are crosschecked with elevation/detail CAD.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_visual_part_contract as mesh; it groups exact CAD refs into future part roles while keeping all geometry blocked.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_plan_detail_crosscheck as mesh readiness unless it has all metric and subgeometry requirements resolved.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_missing_evidence_backlog as a request for user validation; it is an agent-side CAD evidence binding backlog.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_plan_width_candidates as bound opening widths; it only rejects obvious context spans and records plan evidence candidates.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_width_text_crosscheck matches as bound widths; they are numeric-text support candidates only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_width_binding_proposals as final widths; they still need exact detail/wall-cut confirmation before mesh.
Do not promote any width proposal flagged by ground_floor_opening_width_proposal_safety_audit.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_vertical_cut_limit_proposals as cut limits; all missing sill/threshold/header slots must be resolved first.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_p1_resolution_matrix P1 status as mesh readiness.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_vertical_level_context as opening-specific sill/header/threshold binding.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_p1_backlog_rollup candidate-prepared items as final mesh inputs.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_vertical_detail_evidence text as metric slot binding; it is context and search evidence.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_sill_header_candidate_work_order as bound sill/header geometry; it preserves vertical limit candidates and risk flags only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_width_draft_gap_work_order as bound width geometry; it preserves width-gap candidates without promoting opening_width_m.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_strong_width_gap_crosscheck_packets as width bindings; they are review packets for strong missing-width source pairs only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_strong_width_gap_safety_audit as final opening width geometry; eligible means not-final draft slot only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_pending_slot_proposals as bound slots or mesh geometry; it is only a CAD-sourced proposal register combining pending width, sill/header and subgeometry evidence.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_non_wall_cut_exception_contract as deletion; it preserves Y10 cabinetry while removing it from wall-cut blocking.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_pending_slot_resolution_matrix as geometry; it is an agent-side work-priority matrix with zero user-validation and zero mesh-ready rows.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_unresolved_slot_source_search_packets as bound dimensions; they only package existing CAD source pools for the next agent-side search pass.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_exact_cad_measurement_evidence as bound dimensions; it packages exact CAD measurements and source refs for agent-side slot crosscheck only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_slot_role_resolution_work_order as bound dimensions; it is an agent-side role assignment queue with zero mesh-ready packets.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_role_preselection_candidates as bound dimensions; it only isolates CAD-supported candidates for later agent crosscheck.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_role_preselection_safety_audit as mesh; eligible means a not-final source-bound proposal can be made, not a 3D cut.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_not_final_source_bound_slot_candidates as final dimensions; the register is source-bound but still not-final and not mesh-ready.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_source_bound_metric_slot_register as final dimensions; it only consolidates CAD source-bound not-final slots for later exact role contracts.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_y16_rondboog_detail_role_contract as mesh; it separates Y16 CAD subparts and consistency checks only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_y01_arch_window_detail_role_contract as mesh; it preserves Y01 rear arched window CAD dimensions, subparts and context exclusions only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_y02_salon_window_detail_role_contract as mesh; it preserves Y02 salon window CAD width, height, fixed glass, laths and context exclusions only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_y03_sliding_door_detail_role_contract as mesh; it preserves Y03 sliding-door CAD width, leaf subparts and context exclusions only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_y04_hobby_window_detail_role_contract as mesh; it preserves Y04 hobby-room window CAD width, panel/lath subparts and context exclusions only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_y09_y11_small_window_detail_role_contract as mesh; it preserves Y09/Y11 small-window frame, leaf and lath CAD subparts only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_y06_y07_y14_door_plan_role_contract as walkable route or cut mesh; it preserves exact door/portal plan subroles and blockers only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_y01_not_final_cut_context_contract as a wall cut; it combines Y01 not-final slots, cutline and wall context while final mesh remains blocked.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_not_final_slot_coverage_matrix as final cut readiness; it only compares required slots with not-final candidates.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_y16_wall_context_contract as a deletion instruction; it preserves side wall pieces and marks the void span not-final.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_inspector_payload as mesh; it is popup/edit-panel metadata only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_cut_contract as mesh; it separates CAD detail sources from wall/structure sources and keeps all openings blocked until exact cut slots are resolved.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_wall_cut_alignment_contract as mesh; it aligns exact CAD wall context, cutline candidates and visual refs but all openings remain blocked.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_cut_profile_workbench as mesh; it is only an agent-side ranking of source evidence before exact cut-profile binding.
Do not treat ground_floor_interior_opening_wall_context_candidates as accepted cut geometry; it only suggests wall-run candidates from shared original CAD source IDs.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_source_part_graph_contract as mesh; it preserves per-opening CAD part source geometry for later selectable parts only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_elevation_slot_geometry_candidates as boxes or mesh; its envelopes are measurement/indexing evidence around exact CAD segments only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_elevation_subpart_narrowing_workbench as final geometry; connected component envelopes are candidate evidence only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_arch_bay_subpart_workbench as final geometry; Y01/Y05 arch halves and Y17 erker subsegments are CAD source evidence only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_y17_erker_leaf_workbench as final frame/glass/door geometry; it preserves five CAD leaf candidates but leaves the operable raamdeur unresolved.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_subgeometry_slot_candidates as bound geometry; it only prepares Y05 arch_geometry and Y17 bay_subsegment_geometry candidates.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_coordinate_measurements as final dimensions; they are exact CAD coordinate measurements of candidate subentities and still need detail/facade crosscheck.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_width_reconciliation as final dimensions unless a future version explicitly promotes clear source-bound slots.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_cutline_narrowing_workbench as final width binding; close matches still require detail/facade crosscheck.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_width_narrowed_candidates as mesh-ready; they are candidate width slots only and remain blocked by vertical/detail gates.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_vertical_slot_derivation_candidates as final height slots; derived values need facade/detail crosscheck.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_candidate_readiness_matrix as mesh readiness; it is an input/blocker overview only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_focused_crosscheck_packets as validation-approved geometry; they only prioritize the next CAD detail pass.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_metric_candidate_packets as final mesh dimensions; they are complete candidate packets that still require detail/wall-cut confirmation.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_vertical_search_packets as bound vertical slots; they are search pools only.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_vertical_search_classification as bound geometry; it only groups candidate CAD dimensions by likely role.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_vertical_slot_candidate_selector as final geometry; it ranks candidate sill/header pairs only.
Do not generate opening wall cuts while ground_floor_opening_mesh_blocker_matrix reports zero mesh-ready openings.
Do not bypass ground_floor_opening_exact_binding_backlog; every open slot must be resolved from CAD refs before mesh.
Do not treat ground_floor_opening_bound_metric_dimension_draft as final; it only consolidates candidate-bound slots.
Semantic object worksets must validate with zero missing source items and zero geometry/source-ID mismatches before mesh generation.
next_required_steps: Use the original detailed ground_floor_plan CAD catalog as the wall/opening source, not the old simplified wall-segment artifact.
Use facade, section and detail catalog entities to enrich joined objects with levels, openings, material notes and construction details.
Validate detail_enrichment_manifest family-by-family before promoting joined objects to mesh-ready objects.
Use detail_enrichment_qa_boards to decide which source pools need manual narrowing before object-level matching.
Use validated_joined_objects as the next source contract for site basis and building detail matching.
Validate cad_detail_components per source drawing before matching component candidates to joined objects.
Use visible_site_mesh as a regenerated preview output only; the user-validated visible_site_3d_scope remains the master visible site contract.
Use ground_floor_wall_mass_preview_mesh as a regenerated QA preview only; it has exact CAD wall polygons but intentionally uncut openings.
Use first_floor_wall_mass_preview_mesh as a regenerated QA preview only; it has exact CAD wall polygons but intentionally uncut openings and roof joins.
Use first_floor_wall_source_contract as the first-floor wall source layer; do not use preview mesh faces as the modelling source.
Do not generate first-floor room polygons from first_floor_room_seed_wall_context_workbench; it only links CAD label seeds to nearby CAD wall loops.
Do not treat first_floor_wall_graph_workbench as mesh; it is an exact CAD wall-edge graph for future first-floor room-cycle detection only.
Do not treat first_floor_room_boundary_candidate_workbench as room geometry; it is side evidence around labels only and has zero closed or mesh-ready room boundaries.
Do not treat first_floor_opening_plan_source_candidates as windows, doors, voids or mesh; FFC clusters are preserved CAD source linework until facade/section/detail matching.
Do not treat first_floor_opening_elevation_source_candidates as windows, doors, voids or mesh; FEC clusters are upper/unmatched facade source pools until plan/detail matching.
Do not treat first_floor_opening_plan_elevation_match_candidates as opening identities or mesh; it is a review-only relationship workbench between preserved FFC and FEC source clusters.
Do not treat first_floor_opening_evidence_packets as opening identities or mesh; they are source dossiers for later exact CAD subpart/dimension narrowing.
Do not bypass first_floor_opening_exact_binding_backlog; every first-floor opening slot must be resolved from exact CAD refs before mesh.
Do not treat first_floor_opening_dimension_text_source_pools as bound dimensions; they are nearby CAD source pools only and remain not-bound/not-mesh.
Do not treat first_floor_opening_numeric_text_candidate_index values as metric dimensions; units and slots are unknown until exact CAD dimension context is bound.
Do not treat first_floor_opening_numeric_dimension_pair_candidates as bound dimensions; text-to-line proximity is only search evidence until exact slots are selected.
Do not treat first_floor_opening_dimension_pair_readiness_matrix as validation or mesh readiness; it only orders the next exact CAD source-binding pass.
Do not treat first_floor_opening_pair_slot_hint_selector as dimension binding; slot hints are search-only and remain blocked from mesh.
Do not treat first_floor_opening_exact_slot_crosscheck_packets as bound dimensions; unit hypotheses and CAD lengths are crosscheck-only until a later exact slot contract promotes them.
Do not treat first_floor_opening_metric_slot_proposals as accepted dimensions; they are proposals only and still blocked from mesh.
Do not bypass first_floor_opening_metric_proposal_safety_audit; blocked/context proposals must not become metric slots.
Do not generate first-floor opening geometry while first_floor_opening_mesh_blocker_matrix reports zero mesh-ready packets.
Do not generate roof mesh from roof_source_contract until roof plane, edge, ridge, eave, height and overhang roles are exact-source-bound.
Do not generate roof mesh from roof_plan_role_workbench; it only matches exact CAD hatch boundaries to candidate CAD roof lines and keeps final roles blocked.
Do not generate roof mesh from roof_height_slope_evidence_contract; it only collects CAD text/detail evidence and binds zero roof plane levels/slopes.
Do not generate roof mesh while roof_mesh_blocker_matrix reports zero mesh-ready roof planes.
Do not generate room boxes from room_label_anchor_contract; it is hidden label/popup metadata until exact CAD room polygons are derived.
Do not generate room floor/volume meshes from room labels or side evidence while room_mesh_blocker_matrix reports all rooms blocked.
Do not generate room polygons from room_seed_wall_context_workbench; it only links CAD label seeds to nearby CAD wall refs.
Do not treat ground_floor_room_boundary_candidate_workbench as room geometry; it is side evidence around labels only and has zero closed or mesh-ready room boundaries.
Use room_boundary_cycle_search_packets only to focus the next exact CAD cycle pass; closed selected subgraphs are not accepted room polygons.
Use ground_floor_room_portal_candidate_contract only as CAD evidence for future room connectivity; do not create walkable routes until portal sides and wall gaps are exact-bound.
Use ground_floor_portal_side_resolution_work_order only as a CAD binding priority list; it has zero walkable-ready and zero mesh-ready portal candidates.
Use ground_floor_portal_side_source_search_packets only to focus exact CAD side binding; overlaps are not accepted room sides.
Use ground_floor_portal_side_proximity_search_packets only to rank the next exact CAD side-binding search; broad bbox hits and nearest rooms are not accepted room sides.
Use ground_floor_portal_side_evidence_matrix only as the combined portal-side evidence register; promote nothing until exact room-cycle gap edges and exterior thresholds are bound.
Use ground_floor_portal_gap_pair_candidates only to prioritize exact gap-pair crosschecks; never as final walkthrough connectivity.
Use ground_floor_portal_gap_edge_crosscheck_packets only to narrow exact graph gap edges and topology wall runs; never as final walkthrough connectivity.
Use ground_floor_portal_gap_edge_narrowing_work_order only to group gap-edge candidates by exact CAD wall run; never as final walkthrough connectivity.
Use room_editable_object_contract as the future selectable-room metadata register only; do not place decor/furniture or apply room materials until room boundaries are exact-bound.
Use room_mesh_blocker_matrix as the authoritative no-mesh gate for all room floors and room volumes until closed CAD room cycles and door/portal gaps are exact-bound.
Keep site_hardscape_reisolation_workbench as the source-linked split workbench behind the visible site scope, not as final mesh geometry.
Generate exact wall/opening objects from original CAD source_entity_ids, hatches, polylines and door/window symbols; keep the old ground_floor_validated_wall_segments only as archived audit evidence.
Use ground_floor_opening_height_matches as the user-confirmed count/type contract for the 12 ground-floor openings.
Use ground_floor_validated_opening_plan_matches as the editable opening object contract layer, not as final mesh geometry.
Narrow each Y opening object into exact CAD subroles: surrounding wall/column, void, frame, glass/panel, sill/threshold, swing/reference-only.
Use ground_floor_opening_subentity_contracts as candidate role evidence, then review ambiguous detail lines before any mesh cut.
Join narrowed opening subroles to facade/section height evidence so openings are not generated floor-to-ceiling unless the drawings say so.
Treat ground_floor_opening_height_join_contracts as user-validated identity joins only; metric slots must still be bound before mesh.
Use ground_floor_opening_targeted_validation_questions as resolved evidence notes for Y01 and Y09/Y11, not as geometry.
Use ground_floor_opening_metric_dimension_contracts to bind CAD dimension/text entities for width, height, sill, header, threshold, frame depth and glazing subdivision.
Use ground_floor_opening_dimension_entity_binding_candidates as the source-linked CAD dimension/text pool; do not treat candidate values as final dimensions.
Use ground_floor_opening_dimension_slot_binding_workbench to review exact per-opening slot bindings before writing bound dimensions.
Use ground_floor_opening_dimension_line_slot_candidates as text-to-dimension-line evidence only; it does not bind slots or create mesh.
Use facade_source_mapping_audit to select semantic facade role and PDF crop; never infer house side from the raw component filename.
Use ground_floor_opening_dimension_preselection only to make manual CAD dimension selection easier; exact bound source_entity_ids are still required.
Use ground_floor_opening_dimension_human_review as the readable value review page; exact CAD source_entity_ids must still be bound afterwards.
Use ground_floor_architectural_shell_contract as the current house-shell source scope; keep all mesh generation blocked until its readiness gates pass.
Use ground_floor_bound_level_constraints as the source-linked level/material reference contract; do not extrude until these refs are joined to exact wall/roof runs.
Use ground_floor_wall_run_level_join_contract as the current technical wall-run grouping layer; bind openings to these runs before any cut mesh.
Use ground_floor_opening_wall_run_dimension_bindings as the current opening-to-wall-run context layer; it is not a cut geometry layer.
Use ground_floor_opening_3d_preparation_contract as the current opening source-ID split layer before frame/glass/sill/threshold/reveal subrole binding.
Use ground_floor_opening_metric_slot_binding_candidates as CAD text-plus-line evidence only; candidate values are not final dimensions.
Use ground_floor_opening_metric_slot_bindings as the candidate-bound metric evidence layer; preserve context dimensions separately from opening geometry.
Use ground_floor_opening_3d_readiness_contract before any wall cut to verify all required slots and subgeometry are source-bound.
Use ground_floor_opening_plan_trace_subgeometry to narrow exact CAD plan entities into frame, glass, reveal, sill/threshold, wall mass and reference-only roles.
Use ground_floor_opening_plan_subrole_workbench as the conservative candidate split before facade/detail crosscheck.
Use ground_floor_opening_plan_detail_crosscheck to identify the exact missing CAD evidence before promoting opening subgeometry.
Use ground_floor_opening_missing_evidence_backlog to prioritize P1 CAD width and vertical cut-limit binding work.
Use ground_floor_opening_plan_width_candidates to crosscheck plan trace spans against CAD dimension labels before binding opening_width_m.
Use ground_floor_opening_width_text_crosscheck to identify which plan width candidates have matching CAD dimension text before exact binding.
Use ground_floor_opening_width_binding_proposals as the width prebinding source before exact wall-cut confirmation.
Use ground_floor_opening_width_proposal_safety_audit to block likely context spans before any width proposal is promoted.
Use ground_floor_opening_vertical_cut_limit_proposals to carry forward height evidence and list missing sill/threshold/header blockers.
Use ground_floor_opening_p1_resolution_matrix as the current per-opening blocker map for width and vertical cut work.
Use ground_floor_opening_vertical_level_context as general datum/plinth context only while searching for opening-specific vertical slots.
Use ground_floor_opening_p1_backlog_rollup to focus remaining P1 CAD binding work.
Use ground_floor_opening_vertical_detail_evidence as popup/context and as search hints for sill/header/threshold details.
Use ground_floor_opening_inspector_payload to keep labels and source evidence available in future viewer/edit UI.
Use ground_floor_opening_cut_contract to prevent visual opening detail CAD from being mixed with wall/structure CAD when the true 3D cut layer is generated.
Use ground_floor_opening_coordinate_measurements to compare exact CAD-coordinate spans against dimension text and detail CAD before binding final widths.
Use ground_floor_opening_width_reconciliation to identify which opening widths still need narrower source subentity selection.
Use ground_floor_opening_cutline_narrowing_workbench to select narrower source CAD refs for width candidates before binding final slots.
Use ground_floor_opening_width_narrowed_candidates as the current source-bound width candidate pool for later exact metric binding.
Use ground_floor_opening_vertical_slot_derivation_candidates to focus sill/threshold/header searches around CAD-derived candidate values.
Use ground_floor_opening_candidate_readiness_matrix to prioritize which opening needs width, vertical, cutline or detail crosscheck work next.
Use ground_floor_opening_focused_crosscheck_packets to start with Y02 and the next strongest opening candidates.
Use ground_floor_opening_metric_candidate_packets as the first candidate complete metric source for later final binding after crosscheck.
Use ground_floor_opening_vertical_search_packets to resolve missing sill/header/threshold slots for the next width-ready openings.
Use ground_floor_opening_vertical_search_classification to pick plausible vertical slot candidates before exact binding.
Use ground_floor_opening_vertical_slot_candidate_selector to create a small facade-local review set before binding exact vertical slots.
Use ground_floor_opening_mesh_blocker_matrix as the authoritative no-mesh gate for ground-floor openings.
Work through ground_floor_opening_exact_binding_backlog in priority order before creating final cut geometry.
Use ground_floor_opening_bound_metric_dimension_draft as the consolidated candidate slot register for the next binding pass.
Generate ground_floor_opening_bound_metric_dimensions only after exact source_entity_ids are selected per dimension slot.
Generate a source-linked intermediate mesh only after building_detail_matches are validated, not from bounding boxes.
Validate loop extraction in the QA viewer against real CAD/PDF underlays.
Only then generate the walkable WebGL shell and start material/landscape/furniture workflows.
